sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try a systematic approach: click each red block once in sequence
# and check if level completes after each click

# Try clicking top red, middle red, bottom red (each once)
for row, col in [(17, 20), (26, 14), (35, 14)]:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print(f"Level completed after clicking ({row}, {col})!")
        break
    print(f"After ({row},{col}): step={current_frame.step}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}")

# If not completed, try clicking green blocks too
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    for row, col in [(17, 39), (26, 48), (35, 48)]:
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print(f"Level completed after clicking ({row}, {col})!")
            break
        print(f"After ({row},{col}): step={current_frame.step}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}")

# If still not completed, try clicking all 6 in sequence multiple times
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    for iteration in range(3):
        for row, col in [(17, 20), (26, 14), (35, 14), (17, 39), (26, 48), (35, 48)]:
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
            if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
                print(f"Level completed at iteration {iteration+1}, click ({row}, {col})!")
                break
            if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
                print(f"Game over at iteration {iteration+1}, click ({row}, {col})")
                break
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('game_over'):
            break
    else:
        print("No completion after 3 full cycles of 6 clicks")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me try a systematic approach: click each red block once in sequence
# and check if level completes after each click

# Try clicking top red, middle red, bottom red (each once)
for row, col in [(17, 20), (26, 14), (35, 14)]:
    action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
    if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        print(f"Level completed after clicking ({row}, {col})!")
        break
    print(f"After ({row},{col}): step={current_frame.step}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}")

# If not completed, try clicking green blocks too
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    for row, col in [(17, 39), (26, 48), (35, 48)]:
        action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
            print(f"Level completed after clicking ({row}, {col})!")
            break
        print(f"After ({row},{col}): step={current_frame.step}, done={last_action_result.get('done')}")

# If still not completed, try clicking all 6 in sequence multiple times
if not last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
    for iteration in range(3):
        for row, col in [(17, 20), (26, 14), (35, 14), (17, 39), (26, 48), (35, 48)]:
            action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col}])
            if last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
                print(f"Level completed at iteration {iteration+1}, click ({row}, {col})!")
                break
            if last_action_result.get('game_over'):
                print(f"Game over at iteration {iteration+1}, click ({row}, {col})")
                break
        if last_action_result.get('level_completed') or last_action_result.get('game_over'):
            break
    else:
        print("No completion after 3 full cycles of 6 clicks")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
